Cooling Fan Controls Component Location Index
The A/C condenser fan runs at low speed, but it does not run at high speed when the engine coolant temperature is above 96.5 °C (206 °F)

With the A/C off (if equipped) and the engine coolant temperature at 96.5 °C (206 °F) or below, the radiator fan runs at high speed and the A/C condenser fan does not run. When the engine coolant temperature is above 96.5 °C (206 °F), both fans run at high speed
Both the radiator fan and the A/C condenser fan run at high speed with the ignition switch ON (II), the A/C off (if equipped), and the engine coolant temperature below 94.5 °C (202 °F)
Both the radiator fan and the A/C condenser fan run at low speed with the ignition switch ON (II) and the A/C off (if equipped)
With A/C: Both the radiator fan and the A/C condenser fan do not run at low speed with the A/C on
Both the radiator fan and the A/C condenser fan run at low speed, but radiator fan does not run at high speed when the engine coolant temperature is above 96.5 °C (206 °F)

Charging system indicator does not come on with the ignition switch in ON (II)
Radiator and A/C Condenser Fan Motor Test
Cooling Fan Controls Component Location Index
The A/C condenser fan runs at low speed, but it does not run at high speed when the engine coolant temperature is above 96.5 °C (206 °F)

With the A/C off (if equipped) and the engine coolant temperature at 96.5 °C (206 °F) or below, the radiator fan run at high speed and the A/C condenser fan does not run. When the engine coolant temperature is above 96.5 °C (206 °F), both fans run at high speed
Both the radiator fan and the A/C condenser fan run at high speed with the ignition switch in ON (II), the A/C off (if equipped), and the engine coolant temperature below 94.5 °C (202 °F)
Both the radiator fan and the A/C condenser fan run at low speed with the ignition switch in ON (II) and the A/C off (if equipped)
With A/C: Both the radiator fan and the A/C condenser fan do not run at low speed with the A/C on
Both the radiator fan and the A/C condenser fan run at low speed, but radiator fan does not run at high speed when the engine coolant temperature is above 96.5 °C (206 °F)
Both the radiator fan and the A/C condenser fan do not run at high speed when the engine coolant temperature is above 96.5 °C (206 °F)
